key_insight: dpti automates thermodynamic integration workflows to compute phase diagrams with machine learning interatomic potentials, reducing the manual effort of setting up reversible free-energy calculations.
engineering_relevance: The package streamlines free-energy and coexistence-point calculations for solids and liquids, making MLIP-based phase diagram prediction more practical for materials design.
method: Builds reversible thermodynamic integration paths from analytically known reference systems to MLIP-described phases.
Automatically generates, runs, and manages the required molecular dynamics tasks from JSON inputs.
Computes free-energy contributions, estimates uncertainties, and propagates coexistence points into phase boundaries.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstration on two Deep Potential case studies: a silica phase diagram (beta-quartz, coesite, melt) and the ice Ih–liquid water boundary.
watch_item: Accurate results still depend on carefully designed reversible paths and reliable MD sampling, so setup quality and convergence remain important.
paper_title: dpti: An Automated Thermodynamic Integration Workflow for Phase Diagram Calculations with Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
